behavior_summary: When the mirror is struck (U8P_MIRROR::gotHit), the script prepares a process context and schedules a separate 'release' handler instead of performing the full effect inline.
The scheduled MIRROR::release scans a collection of nearby/related items to detect whether any NPCs are present.
If any NPC is found, release updates a subset of items by setting a visual/state bit (frame |= 0x04) on each matched item; if no NPC is present it masks frames to keep only low bits (frame &= 0x03).
Overall effect appears to toggle or update visual frames/states of nearby items based on NPC presence when the mirror 'releases' after being hit.
notable_control_flow: Entry via Event 6 (U8P_MIRROR::gotHit) — prepares process info and excludes the process from normal processing, then spawns the release handler and returns/suspends.

U8P_MIRROR::MIRROR::release performs two passes: a scan to detect NPC presence (sets a local flag), then a second loop that updates item frames with one of two branches (OR with 0x04 vs AND with 0x03) based on that flag.
The heavy work (iteration + frame updates) is offloaded to a spawned process (spawn) rather than executed in the initial hit handler, preventing inline blocking.
open_questions: What exact set/collection of items is iterated by release (nearby tile, container, the mirror's children, or global registry)?
What concrete gameplay semantics do the frame bit 0x04 and the mask 0x03 represent visually or mechanically?
What precisely do the U8P_MIRROR::gotHit parameters represent (attacker id, damage, hit type)? They are forwarded to the spawned release but their use inside release is not visible in the excerpt.
Are there additional side-effects or global flags outside this excerpt that interact with the mirror's release sequence (e.g., quest flags, persistent state)?

main_function.name: U8P_MIRROR::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Respond to the mirror being hit by preparing a dedicated process context and spawning MIRROR::release to perform the release effect asynchronously.
entry_conditions: Event 6 (U8P_MIRROR::gotHit) — invoked when the mirror receives a hit/attack.

function_map.name: U8P_MIRROR::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle incoming hit events, set up process context/process exclusion and spawn the release handler instead of executing the release inline.

function_map.name: U8P_MIRROR::release
function_map.role: Scan related items for NPC presence and update those items' frames to either set bit 0x04 (if NPC present) or mask with 0x03 (if no NPC), then finish.

internal_calls.purpose: Schedule/spawn the release handler to run the mirror's release behavior asynchronously under the current process context.

external_calls.purpose: Detect whether an iterated item is an NPC; used to set a local flag controlling which frame-update branch runs.
Read the current frame/index of an item so it can be modified before writing back.
Write the modified frame/index back to the item (applies OR 0x04 or AND 0x03 result).
System call to create/schedule the MIRROR::release process instead of running the release inline.
Engine-level operations used to set up execution context and exclude the process from normal processing before or while spawning the release handler.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the mirror is struck (U8P_MIRROR::gotHit), the script prepares a process context and schedules a separate 'release' handler instead of performing the full effect inline.
